content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Little Elephant and Interval". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/204/problem/A
content.inputFormat: The input consists of four integers: a, b, c, d, where a, b, c, d are the boundaries of two intervals.
content.outputFormat: Print the length of the overlap between the two intervals. If the intervals do not overlap, print 0.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Calculate the maximum of the start points and the minimum of the end points. If the maximum of the start points is less than or equal to the minimum of the end points, the intervals overlap.

code.Python3:
a, b, c, d = map(int, input().split())
maxStart = max(a, c)
minEnd = min(b, d)
if maxStart <= minEnd:
    print(minEnd - maxStart + 1)
else:
    print(0)
